title: DNS module not found despite environment variables set
symptom: When trying to require the 'dns' module in a Node.js function, n8n throws 'Cannot find module 'dns''. This occurs even after setting the appropriate environment variables.
not_this_if: The error does not occur when using other built-in modules
root_cause: The error occurs because the environment variables may not be properly inherited by the worker processes in the Docker container. Even if the variables are set, they might not be applied correctly in the execution context of the Node.js function.
fix_steps: Check if the environment variables are correctly set in the Docker container
Restart the Docker container to ensure the new environment variables are applied
Test the module availability again using the Execute node
agent_instruction: Verify and restart the Docker container to apply the environment variable changes.
